automotive-ev-battery.title: Thailand issues stop-sale order for Volvo EX30 over fire risk
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Thailand's Department of Land Transport has issued a stop-sale order for the Volvo EX30 following multiple fire incidents involving the model in the country. Volvo Cars Thailand has responded by removing the vehicle from its consumer website, signaling serious safety concerns that could affect the automaker's market position and reputation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: GreenWay raises €138 million for CEE charging network
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Polish charging point operator GreenWay has secured €138 million in debt financing from a consortium of European financial institutions to expand its EV charging network across Central and Eastern Europe. This significant investment reflects growing infrastructure development to support electric vehicle adoption in the region.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Europe EV Sales Report: BEVs Jump 42% and Reach 23% Market Share
automotive-ev-battery.summary: European battery electric vehicle registrations surged 42 percent in April, reaching 23 percent market share with nearly 385,000 plugin vehicles registered. The growth is driven by new model availability, sustained fuel prices, and the arrival of Chinese manufacturers in the market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: MG brings electric premium models IM5 and IM6 to Germany
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MG Motor is expanding its presence in Western Europe by launching the fully electric IM5 saloon and IM6 SUV, featuring 800-volt architecture, across Germany, France, Belgium, and the Netherlands in July. The move represents a significant push by the Chinese manufacturer to establish premium EV credentials in competitive European markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EVs and more: European partners launch own semiconductor initiative
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Moore4Power consortium, led by Infineon and comprising 62 partners, has launched one of Europe's largest semiconductor research projects focused on developing next-generation intelligent power electronics for electric mobility applications. This initiative aims to strengthen Europe's technological independence in critical EV supply chain components.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Polestar and Clever test bidirectional charging
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Polestar and Danish charging provider Clever are conducting a pilot project in Denmark to test bidirectional charging technologies including Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Vehicle-to-Home (V2H) functionality. The initiative will evaluate emergency household power supply during outages, advancing grid flexibility capabilities.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 435 New EV Chargers To Be Installed In Philadelphia
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Philadelphia will collaborate with PositivEnergy to install 435 new public electric vehicle chargers across the city, significantly expanding charging infrastructure accessibility. This major deployment supports the city's commitment to EV adoption and addresses the critical need for public charging network expansion.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Graphite deal with Tesla: Syrah reports resolution in delivery dispute
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Australian resources company Syrah Resources has achieved an interim success in its graphite supply dispute with Tesla, with Tesla withdrawing its termination notice. However, underlying contract uncertainties persist, requiring further resolution of the critical battery material supply agreement.

automotive-ev-battery.title: VW locks API for external charging control
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen has restricted external access to its charging API, preventing third-party applications from monitoring and controlling EV charging processes. The closure affects users who relied on the interface to optimize charging with surplus solar energy from home photovoltaic systems.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mercedes expands electric GLC line-up with two new variants
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz plans to expand the electric GLC lineup beyond the current 360 kW all-wheel-drive model by introducing two additional powertrain variants with a smaller battery option. The automaker is expected to open order books for these new configurations later this month.

science-space.title: Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization'
science-space.summary: Astronomers have discovered a previously unknown population of black hole mergers that could fundamentally change our understanding of how these cosmic titans collide and interact. This discovery represents a major breakthrough in gravitational wave astronomy and black hole physics.

science-space.title: NASA's X-59 jet is ready to break the sound barrier for the 1st time this month
science-space.summary: NASA's experimental X-59 aircraft is prepared to achieve its first supersonic flight this month, marking a significant milestone in aeronautical research. The test will advance NASA's capabilities in studying supersonic flight phenomena.

science-space.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
science-space.summary: Engineers at NASA's Goddard Space Flight Center have completed the final inspection of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope's 7.9-foot primary mirror, which will enable the observatory to capture panoramic images of distant cosmic objects. This milestone brings the telescope closer to its operational deployment.

science-space.title: NASA Invites Media to See Roman Space Telescope Arrive at Kennedy
science-space.summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is scheduled to arrive at Kennedy Space Center in Florida following completion of construction and testing at Goddard Space Flight Center. Media are invited to document the historic arrival of this major space observatory.

science-space.title: How do you study an invisible exoplanet? Astronomers discover planetary 'fingerprints' in the rings around stars
science-space.summary: Astronomers have developed a novel method to detect and characterize invisible exoplanets by analyzing their gravitational signatures in the debris rings surrounding stars. This breakthrough technique expands the toolkit for discovering and studying planets that cannot be directly observed.

science-space.title: Shockwaves from dying stars may sculpt 'cosmic wagon wheel' stellar nurseries, simulations reveal
science-space.summary: Three-dimensional simulations demonstrate how shockwaves from stellar explosions and solar winds may create distinctive hub-and-spoke structures in molecular clouds. These findings provide new insights into star formation processes across the Milky Way.

science-space.title: Rocket goes boom, satellite cameras zoom: Explosive Blue Origin damage is visible from space
science-space.summary: Satellite imagery has captured detailed views of the damage sustained by Blue Origin's LC-36 launchpad following the New Glenn rocket explosion. The high-resolution observations document the extent of infrastructure damage visible from orbit.

science-space.title: Pretty in Pink
science-space.summary: A new composite image of the young star cluster Westerlund 2 combines X-ray data from the Chandra Observatory and infrared observations from the James Webb Space Telescope, revealing stars between one and three million years old. The visualization highlights the stunning complexity of stellar nurseries through multi-wavelength astronomy.

science-space.title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
science-space.summary: NASA has selected seven companies to undertake construction and infrastructure improvements at Johnson Space Center in Houston, with contracts supporting up to $300 million in upgrades. The project will enhance mission-support facilities, utilities, and equipment across the NASA Johnson campus by September 2026.

science-space.title: Gravity Waves From Super Typhoon Sinlaku
science-space.summary: Satellite observations have documented striking atmospheric phenomena in the upper atmosphere generated by the intensifying tropical cyclone Super Typhoon Sinlaku. The detection of gravity waves from the storm provides valuable data for understanding atmospheric dynamics and extreme weather systems.

finance-markets.title: Alphabet to sell $80bn in stock to fund AI spending spree
finance-markets.summary: Alphabet announced plans to raise $80 billion through stock sales to finance its artificial intelligence expansion, including a $10 billion private placement from Berkshire Hathaway. This landmark capital raise underscores the technology sector's massive investment requirements to compete in the AI arms race and signals confidence from major institutional investors in the company's AI strategy.

finance-markets.title: Anthropic files for blockbuster initial public offering
finance-markets.summary: Anthropic has filed for an initial public offering, intensifying competition with OpenAI and SpaceX for capital and market validation in the artificial intelligence sector. The move tests Wall Street's appetite for major private AI companies seeking to go public amid the continued technology investment boom.

finance-markets.title: HPE shares soar 37% on booming demand for AI infrastructure
finance-markets.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise's shares surged 37 percent following strong demand for data centre equipment, with sales of servers and networking equipment rising rapidly. The rally reflects investor enthusiasm for companies providing essential infrastructure to support the widespread deployment of artificial intelligence systems.

finance-markets.title: South Korea Overtakes India as World's Sixth-Largest Stock Market
finance-markets.summary: South Korea's equity market has surpassed India's to become the world's sixth-largest, driven by exceptional performance from semiconductor companies leading the global artificial intelligence buildout. The shift reflects the increasing market value concentration in technology-driven economies capitalizing on AI infrastructure demand.

finance-markets.title: Trump claims Israel and Hizbollah agree ceasefire after Iran threats
finance-markets.summary: President Trump announced that Israel and Hizbollah have agreed to a ceasefire following tensions with Iran, though Tehran has signaled it is suspending peace talks with the United States. The geopolitical uncertainty has driven oil prices higher as markets assess the implications for Persian Gulf energy supplies.

finance-markets.title: Oil Holds Biggest Gain in a Month on Standoff in US-Iran Talks
finance-markets.summary: Crude oil steadied after posting its largest monthly gain amid uncertainty surrounding ongoing United States-Iran peace negotiations and the risk of disrupted energy flows from the Persian Gulf. Market participants remain cautious as diplomatic efforts continue with potential implications for global energy prices.

finance-markets.title: Elliott Buys Stake in Top Australia Gold Miner Northern Star
finance-markets.summary: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has acquired a stake in Northern Star Resources, Australia's largest gold miner, and is advocating for a strategic review that could include a potential asset sale. The move signals Elliott's confidence in the value realization potential within the precious metals sector.

finance-markets.title: Billionaire Kazakh Brokerage CEO Gets SEC Enforcement Warning
finance-markets.summary: The billionaire chief executive of Kazakhstan-based Freedom Holding Corp. has received a Wells Notice from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission indicating the regulator may pursue enforcement action. The development raises questions about regulatory compliance at the international financial services firm.

finance-markets.title: Short seller Andrew Left found guilty of securities fraud
finance-markets.summary: Prominent short seller Andrew Left has been convicted of securities fraud, a verdict with potential ramifications for investors and commentators who publish research on publicly traded companies. The case may reshape legal standards around investment commentary and short-selling practices.

finance-markets.title: Chinese Curbs Risk Derailing Record Hong Kong Home-Buying Spree
finance-markets.summary: Chinese mainland buyers who achieved record spending on Hong Kong real estate in the first quarter now face obstacles from stricter regulations limiting capital outflows by wealthy individuals. The new restrictions could significantly constrain the momentum in Hong Kong's property market that benefited from sustained mainland investment.

world-news.title: Four dead and dozens injured after massive Russian strikes across Ukraine
world-news.summary: Russian forces launched ballistic missile strikes on Kyiv's city center, killing at least four people and injuring dozens as thousands of residents sought shelter. The attack represents a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Trump talks to Hezbollah, Israel as Lebanon fighting surges
world-news.summary: Diplomatic efforts intensify as the Trump administration engages with both Israel and Hezbollah, while the death toll from Israel's military operations in Lebanon since March has reached 3,433. The escalating regional conflict poses significant risks to Middle Eastern stability.

world-news.title: Lebanon says Hezbollah agrees reciprocal halt to attacks on Israel
world-news.summary: Lebanon announced that Hezbollah has agreed to a reciprocal ceasefire with Israel, though the Israeli military subsequently reported intercepting projectiles from Lebanese territory. The potential pause marks a critical development in ongoing hostilities between the two adversaries.

world-news.title: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
world-news.summary: Under domestic political pressure and appeals from Gulf allies, the Trump administration is pushing for a settlement in the Iran conflict, yet Iranian leadership remains firm in demanding concessions. The diplomatic impasse threatens to prolong regional instability.

world-news.title: Denmark gets new government as Greenland crisis persists
world-news.summary: Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen has formed a new center-left coalition government following months of negotiations, while managing the geopolitical challenge of Trump's stated interest in acquiring Greenland. The Danish government must balance domestic governance with international diplomatic pressure.

world-news.title: Celebration, shock and scepticism follow Colombia's presidential election
world-news.summary: Colombia's first round of presidential voting has produced an unexpected result, with far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella advancing to a runoff against left-wing Senator Ivan Cepeda. The outcome signals a potential rightward political shift in the South American nation.

world-news.title: Three Ebola vaccines in development amid growing outbreak fears
world-news.summary: Multiple international organizations including IAVI, Moderna, and the University of Oxford are accelerating vaccine development efforts as global Ebola outbreak concerns intensify. The coordinated international response aims to prevent a major public health crisis.

world-news.title: Hungarian PM threatens to oust Orbán-era president
world-news.summary: Prime Minister Péter Magyar has demanded the resignation of Hungary's president, who has refused to step aside, precipitating a constitutional crisis within the European Union member state. The conflict reflects broader political tensions over governmental structure and democratic governance.

world-news.title: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
world-news.summary: Denmark's Social Democratic Party leader Mette Frederiksen has secured a center-left coalition minority government following extended negotiations, securing her third term as prime minister. The formation of this government resolves a period of electoral deadlock in the Nordic nation.

world-news.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters clash with Israeli police over army draft
world-news.summary: Ultra-Orthodox Jewish protesters in Jerusalem blocked roads and clashed with police over military conscription policies, highlighting internal tensions within Israeli society regarding mandatory military service. The demonstrations reflect ongoing disputes over the role of religious communities in national security obligations.

tech-ai.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80B to pay for AI buildout
tech-ai.summary: Alphabet announced plans to raise $80 billion to fund its artificial intelligence infrastructure expansion, citing strong enterprise and consumer demand for its AI solutions that is currently exceeding available supply. The massive capital commitment underscores the company's commitment to maintaining competitive advantage in the rapidly growing AI market.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has officially filed for an initial public offering with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, achieving a post-money valuation of $965 billion as of its latest fundraise. The filing marks a significant milestone in the AI industry and positions Anthropic ahead of rival OpenAI in the race to become a publicly traded company.

tech-ai.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
tech-ai.summary: Nvidia is expanding into the consumer laptop processor market with its RTX Spark AI agent chips in partnership with Microsoft, Dell, and HP, targeting the $200 billion CPU market. The move represents a significant competitive challenge to Qualcomm's dominance in Windows laptops and could deliver improved performance and AI capabilities to mainstream consumers.

tech-ai.title: Hackers hijacked Instagram accounts by tricking Meta AI support chatbot into granting access
tech-ai.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support chatbot to gain unauthorized access to Instagram accounts by requesting email changes and password resets on behalf of legitimate users. The vulnerability, which has since been patched by Meta, highlighted critical security gaps in AI-driven customer support systems and affected high-profile accounts including former President Barack Obama's White House Instagram.

tech-ai.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
tech-ai.summary: Florida has filed a landmark lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman alleging that ChatGPT played a role in violent incidents, including a shooting at Florida State University. The first-of-its-kind legal action raises significant questions about AI companies' liability for potential harms related to their generative AI systems.

tech-ai.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
tech-ai.summary: Defense technology startup Mach Industries raised an additional $300 million in funding, elevating its valuation to $1.8 billion, a fourfold increase in a single year under 22-year-old CEO Ethan Thornton. The company has five autonomous vehicles under development and completed a major acquisition as it captures growing investor and government interest in advanced defense technologies.

tech-ai.title: Gemini's new AI agent is about as good as Google's demo
tech-ai.summary: Google's Gemini Spark AI agent can execute multi-step tasks autonomously in the background, delivering performance similar to company demonstrations, but raises questions about cost and privacy implications. The agent showcases significant capability advances in AI automation while presenting trade-offs between convenience and user data protection.

tech-ai.title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX's IPO
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX has identified water access as a significant risk factor in its upcoming initial public offering, noting that its data center operations require substantial and affordable water resources for cooling. The disclosure underscores the substantial infrastructure challenges facing companies expanding AI and computing capabilities at scale.

tech-ai.title: This could be Windows' M1 moment — but expect it to cost a ton
tech-ai.summary: Nvidia's entry into consumer laptop processors with RTX Spark chips represents a potential inflection point for Windows computing similar to Apple's M1 breakthrough, though pricing is expected to be premium. The move could finally deliver competitive Arm-based performance in graphics-intensive applications, a domain where Qualcomm has struggled.

tech-ai.title: Meta's own AI was exploited to hijack Instagram accounts
tech-ai.summary: Meta's AI support chatbot was weaponized by hackers to compromise Instagram accounts through social engineering, demonstrating how language models can be manipulated to bypass security protocols. The incident, which Meta addressed through a patch, underscores the emerging security challenges posed by delegating account management responsibilities to AI systems.

health-biotech.title: Abivax ulcerative colitis drug shows strong efficacy, but cases of cancer raise concerns
health-biotech.summary: Abivax reported that its experimental ulcerative colitis treatment demonstrated significant efficacy in a closely watched clinical trial, though the company's stock declined sharply due to emerging safety concerns related to cancer cases. The trial results represent a potential advancement in inflammatory bowel disease treatment, but the safety profile requires careful evaluation before regulatory approval.

health-biotech.title: #ASCO26: Kelonia Therapeutics delivers an impressive in vivo CAR-T update
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly's recent $7 billion acquisition of Kelonia Therapeutics is validated by promising clinical data showing that all 18 multiple myeloma patients treated with Kelonia's in vivo CAR-T therapy responded positively. The breakthrough results underscore the potential of next-generation cell therapies in hematologic malignancies and justify the substantial investment.

health-biotech.title: Servier signs $1.55B upfront deal for Edgewise's muscular dystrophy assets
health-biotech.summary: French pharmaceutical company Servier acquired Edgewise Therapeutics' muscular dystrophy business for $1.55 billion upfront, gaining access to an experimental neuromuscular medicine expected to generate pivotal data by year's end. The major licensing deal reflects strong commercial interest in neuromuscular disease treatments and positions Servier for potential regulatory success in this therapeutic area.

health-biotech.title: Epigenetic editing shows promise against chronic hep B infections
health-biotech.summary: Tune Therapeutics reported promising results for a novel CRISPR-based epigenetic editing approach that silences disease-causing genes without altering underlying DNA to treat chronic hepatitis B infections. This innovative mechanism represents a potential paradigm shift in gene editing therapy by avoiding permanent DNA modifications while still achieving therapeutic benefit.

health-biotech.title: #ASCO26: AstraZeneca's triplet combo succeeds in liver cancer subgroup
health-biotech.summary: AstraZeneca announced positive data from a study demonstrating that a combination of a targeted treatment and two immunotherapies can benefit patients with specific subtypes of liver cancer. The triplet combination approach reflects the oncology field's growing focus on multi-modal therapeutic strategies to improve outcomes in solid tumors.

health-biotech.title: Global coalition to fast-track three vaccines targeting Ebola outbreak with $62 million in funding
health-biotech.summary: A global health coalition has secured $62 million in funding to accelerate development of at least three vaccines against the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Congo, where no licensed vaccines are currently available. The coordinated international effort aims to rapidly address the urgent public health threat through expedited vaccine development and clinical evaluation.

health-biotech.title: Shionogi wins US approval for first pill to prevent Covid following exposure
health-biotech.summary: Japan's Shionogi received FDA approval for its oral antiviral medication to prevent COVID-19 infection in adults and children over 12 years who have been exposed to the virus. The approval represents a significant advancement in post-exposure prophylaxis, offering an oral alternative to existing prevention strategies.

health-biotech.title: Eli Lilly warns hospitals to submit claims data in the next five days or lose their 340B drug discounts
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly has issued an urgent directive to over 1,000 hospitals participating in the 340B drug discount program to submit claims data within five days or face loss of their negotiated price breaks. The compliance demand highlights ongoing tensions between pharmaceutical manufacturers and healthcare providers regarding program participation and data transparency.

health-biotech.title: Turn Therapeutics' eczema drug shows rapid response in mid-stage data
health-biotech.summary: Turn Therapeutics reported Phase 2 data for its topical atopic dermatitis treatment demonstrating faster-than-typical symptom improvement in an interim analysis of 50 patients. The encouraging results support advancement toward later-stage development for this topical therapy addressing a significant unmet need in dermatology.

health-biotech.title: As FDA misses deadline on electric shock ban, disability advocates speak out
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has failed to meet its self-imposed deadline for banning electrical shock devices used on individuals with intellectual disabilities, prompting renewed advocacy from disability rights groups. The missed deadline underscores ongoing regulatory challenges in addressing potentially harmful practices affecting vulnerable populations.

startups-vc.title: Anthropic Nears $1T Valuation And Leapfrogs OpenAI On Unicorn Board With $65B Funding Round
startups-vc.summary: Generative AI company Anthropic announced a $65 billion Series H funding round, more than doubling its post-money valuation to $965 billion and positioning it ahead of OpenAI in the AI funding race. This mega-round underscores the intense competition among AI behemoths to achieve dominant market positions.

startups-vc.title: Anthropic Files Confidentially For IPO
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic has submitted a confidential IPO filing, escalating the race among generative AI companies to reach the public market first. This move signals the company's readiness to transition from private to public markets amid unprecedented valuations.

startups-vc.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
startups-vc.summary: Defense technology startup Mach Industries, led by 22-year-old founder Ethan Thornton, has raised $300 million in new funding, quadrupling its valuation to $1.8 billion in just one year. The company is advancing five autonomous vehicles and recently completed a major acquisition, establishing itself as a key player in the defense tech sector.

startups-vc.title: Entrata at $575m ARR Growing 23% Is First of the Next Wave of PE-Backed Software IPOs. The Catch: Almost None of Them Are Accelerating.
startups-vc.summary: Entrata, a multifamily property management SaaS platform, filed its S-1 for a NYSE listing with $575 million annual recurring revenue and 23% growth, marking the beginning of a new wave of private equity-backed software company IPOs. However, the company's solid but unspectacular metrics highlight that many PE-backed software firms are facing growth plateaus.

startups-vc.title: They Saw Women Shut Out Of VC, So A PayPal Veteran And Former Navy Officer Built An Alternative
startups-vc.summary: Co-founders Molly Huyck and Amie Konwinski launched Aequitas Invest, a funding portal designed to help women-led businesses raise capital while maintaining greater equity ownership. The platform addresses systemic barriers in venture capital funding by providing an alternative pathway for female entrepreneurs.

startups-vc.title: Crunchbase Data: Venture Dollars For Black Startup Founders Stay Scarce Despite AI Funding Boom
startups-vc.summary: Despite increased overall venture capital activity and an AI funding boom, Crunchbase data shows that the share of U.S. startup funding directed to companies with Black founders remained low in 2025. This persistent disparity highlights ongoing systemic inequities in access to venture capital.

startups-vc.title: Revolut rolls out services to thousands of users in India ahead of broader launch
startups-vc.summary: British fintech startup Revolut has built a waitlist of approximately 450,000 users in India as it prepares for a full market launch. The expansion marks a significant step in the company's international growth strategy in one of the world's largest emerging markets.

startups-vc.title: Unastella, a South Korean rocket startup that launched from home, raises $24M
startups-vc.summary: Seoul-based aerospace startup Unastella has secured $24 million in funding to develop its own launch vehicles and engines. The company's bootstrapped origins and rapid capital raise underscore growing investor interest in the commercial space sector.

startups-vc.title: Navigating The DPI Crunch And Startup Funding
startups-vc.summary: Guest author Marc Schröder advises founders to evaluate investors' financial health alongside their market thesis while preparing for acquisition-focused exits, as venture-backed M&A significantly outweighs IPO activity in the current market. The analysis reflects broader trends in liquidity events for venture-backed companies.

startups-vc.title: Boston Startup Fundraising Looks Strong Only By Pre-AI Parameters
startups-vc.summary: Boston-area startups have attracted approximately $7.8 billion in funding so far this year, putting the region on track for its strongest performance in about four years. However, the growth appears moderate when viewed against historical metrics and broader AI-era funding trends.

crypto-web3.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy sells 32 bitcoin for $2.5 million as total holdings drop to 843,706 BTC
crypto-web3.summary: MicroStrategy sold 32 bitcoin, reducing its holdings to 843,706 BTC and marking its first major liquidation since 2022. The sale represents a significant shift in the company's bitcoin treasury strategy, though its remaining holdings still account for over 4% of the total bitcoin supply.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum's Vitalik Buterin is rethinking how DeFi handles market crashes
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin proposed a new approach to DeFi stability by creating index-tracking assets using options contracts instead of debt-based structures. The proposal addresses systemic risks in current DeFi protocols during market downturns.

crypto-web3.title: Japan's ruling party supports crypto ETF trading, yen-based stablecoins
crypto-web3.summary: Japan's Liberal Democratic Party proposed creating a legal framework to enable crypto ETF trading and yen-denominated stablecoins, signaling regulatory openness toward digital assets. The proposal marks a significant step toward mainstream cryptocurrency adoption in a major developed economy.

crypto-web3.title: Robinhood closes $180 million WonderFi acquisition, entering Canadian crypto market
crypto-web3.summary: Robinhood completed its acquisition of Canadian crypto platform WonderFi for $180 million, gaining control of exchanges Bitbuy and Coinsquare. The deal marks Robinhood's strategic expansion into the Canadian cryptocurrency market and consolidation of regional crypto infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto funds suffer second-largest outflows of 2026 while XRP and HYPE attract inflows
crypto-web3.summary: Digital asset investment products experienced $1.67 billion in outflows last week, with bitcoin funds posting their largest weekly outflow of 2026 according to CoinShares data. Alternative assets like XRP and HYPE tokens bucked the trend by attracting investor capital.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto investment firm Keyrock is acquiring bankrupt lender BlockFills
crypto-web3.summary: Brussels-based digital asset services firm Keyrock plans to acquire Chicago-based BlockFills out of bankruptcy, pending court approval. The acquisition represents a consolidation opportunity in the crypto lending sector following BlockFills' Chapter 11 filing.

crypto-web3.title: TON revives Gram token brand as Telegram CEO Durov says network is 'returning to roots'
crypto-web3.summary: The TON blockchain rebranded its token back to Gram following network upgrades and fee reductions, with Telegram CEO Pavel Durov emphasizing the network's return to its original vision. The rebrand accompanies Telegram's increased involvement in TON governance.

crypto-web3.title: Dogecoin gains access to Paxos network used by PayPal and Venmo
crypto-web3.summary: Dogecoin obtained integration on the Paxos enterprise-grade brokerage and custody network through a partnership with House of Doge. The integration provides DOGE with access to the same infrastructure used by PayPal and Venmo, enhancing its institutional and mainstream adoption potential.

crypto-web3.title: Grayscale sets 0.29% fee for its Hyperliquid ETF, undercutting Bitwise and 21Shares
crypto-web3.summary: Grayscale announced a sponsor fee of 0.29% for its new Hyperliquid exchange-traded fund, expected to launch within the week at a lower cost than competing offerings. The competitive pricing reflects intensifying competition in the crypto ETF market.

crypto-web3.title: Unable to recover from roughly $50 million hack, Radiant Capital is winding down
crypto-web3.summary: Radiant Capital announced it is winding down operations after failing to recover significant funds or raise new capital following a 2024 exploit that cost approximately $50 million. The closure underscores ongoing security challenges and recovery difficulties in the DeFi ecosystem.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group launched a data extortion attack against Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupting classes and coursework across U.S. schools and universities. The attackers defaced the login page with a ransom demand threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: Attackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset account passwords and briefly defaced high-profile Instagram accounts, including those of the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force with pro-Iranian imagery. Instructions for the exploit were circulated on Telegram, demonstrating a vulnerability in AI-powered customer support systems.

cybersecurity.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
cybersecurity.summary: A supply chain attack campaign codenamed Miasma has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines while delivering a self-propagating worm. The attack employs install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, and encrypted exfiltration tactics against the npm ecosystem.

cybersecurity.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers disclosed a malicious supply chain campaign targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a compromised package called codexui-android available on GitHub and npm. The fraudulent tool, which has attracted over 29,000 weekly downloads, is designed to steal OpenAI authentication tokens from unsuspecting developers.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
cybersecurity.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN is being actively exploited in the wild following public disclosure. Adversaries have conducted two attack waves beginning in mid-May, exploiting the flaw under specific conditions to gain unauthorized access.

cybersecurity.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin with over 15,000 sales, to create malicious administrator accounts on compromised websites. The plugin's security flaw allows attackers to gain full control of affected WordPress installations.

cybersecurity.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch law enforcement and the National Cyber Security Center announced the successful takedown of a massive botnet comprising at least 17 million infected devices including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices. More than 200 servers based in the Netherlands were operating as command-and-control infrastructure for the malicious network.

cybersecurity.title: China-Aligned Groups Ramp Up Attacks: Dragon Weave Hits Czech Republic & Taiwan
cybersecurity.summary: A cyber espionage campaign codenamed Operation Dragon Weave is targeting government, research, academic, technology, and financial services officials in the Czech Republic and Taiwan with spear-phishing emails containing malicious ZIP attachments. The campaign delivers an AdaptixC2 remote access agent to compromised systems.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle released record volumes of security patches in May 2026, reflecting AI tools' effectiveness at identifying vulnerabilities in code. The accelerated patching tempo demonstrates how artificial intelligence is improving security vulnerability discovery across the industry.

cybersecurity.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers discovered a vulnerability in OpenAI's ChatGPT that exploits the platform's trust in Markdown links and images to enable prompt injection and phishing attacks. The technique, dubbed ChatGPhish, demonstrates how AI assistants can be weaponized to deliver social engineering attacks through implicit trust mechanisms.

energy-climate.title: AI boom means US is now 'investing more' in fossil-fuel power than China
energy-climate.summary: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence data centers is driving a significant surge in gas-fired power plant investments in the United States, surpassing China's fossil fuel power investments. This trend highlights the growing tension between decarbonization goals and the energy-intensive demands of emerging technology sectors.

energy-climate.title: EM-DAT: Trump aid cuts could close database storing 'world's memory of disasters'
energy-climate.summary: Proposed cuts to U.S. foreign aid threaten to shut down EM-DAT, the world's most comprehensive disaster database relied upon by thousands of climate scientists and policymakers for climate research and disaster analysis. The closure would eliminate critical infrastructure for understanding climate impacts and disaster patterns globally.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 29 May 2026: Europe's 'mind-boggling' May | Indian heat deaths | Nigeria's solar mini-grids
energy-climate.summary: This weekly digest covers major climate and energy developments including unprecedented heat events in Europe, heat-related deaths in India, and renewable energy expansion through solar mini-grids in Nigeria. The report synthesizes critical climate impacts and clean energy solutions across multiple regions.

energy-climate.title: PJM monitor urges FERC to condition Mara power plant buy for data center complex
energy-climate.summary: The grid operator's monitoring analytics division recommended that the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission condition approval of a $1.5 billion power plant acquisition on the buyer maintaining the Long Ridge facility within the PJM Interconnection. The decision reflects concerns about grid reliability amid increasing data center power demands.

energy-climate.title: Tandem PV announces 30.4% efficient perovskite/silicon demonstration module
energy-climate.summary: Tandem PV demonstrated a 100 cm² mini-module achieving 30.4% conversion efficiency using a proprietary perovskite glass layer combined with high-efficiency silicon, with full-size modules expected to reach 28% efficiency later in 2026. This breakthrough represents significant progress toward next-generation solar technology for utility-scale applications.

energy-climate.title: JinkoSolar shipped 86.8 GW of modules in 2025, securing lead in global sales for the seventh time
energy-climate.summary: JinkoSolar maintained its position as the world's leading solar panel manufacturer, shipping 86.8 GW in 2025 and surpassing 400 GW in cumulative shipments by Q1 2026. The company projects that high-power modules exceeding 640 W will comprise more than 60% of its output in 2026, reflecting market trends toward larger, more efficient units.

energy-climate.title: Philadelphia clean energy campaign turned millions invested into billions saved. Here's how.
energy-climate.summary: Philadelphia's integrated clean energy initiative, combining public and private financing with green banking mechanisms and labor partnerships, generated 11,000 jobs and $1.4 billion in energy savings with minimal federal funding. The program demonstrates a replicable model for scaling clean energy investment and community economic benefits.

energy-climate.title: Türkiye adds 1.97 GW of solar in January-April
energy-climate.summary: Turkey installed nearly 2 GW of solar capacity in the first four months of 2026, with the energy minister confirming that solar is on track to surpass hydropower as the country's primary electricity source by year-end. The rapid deployment reflects Turkey's accelerating renewable energy transition.

energy-climate.title: Trina Solar announces 907 W tandem solar module with 29.2% efficiency
energy-climate.summary: Trina Solar unveiled a high-power tandem solar module with 907 W capacity and 29.2% efficiency, independently certified by TÜV SÜD and designed for mass production rather than laboratory conditions. The module represents commercial-scale progress in advanced solar technology suitable for utility deployment.

energy-climate.title: Qualitas Energy funds 211MW battery projects in Italy
energy-climate.summary: Qualitas Energy secured financing for a 211 MW battery energy storage system portfolio in Italy, expanding grid storage capacity to support renewable energy integration. The investment demonstrates growing confidence in battery storage solutions for managing intermittent renewable generation in European markets.

asia-pacific.title: Why oil-hungry Asia ignores risks linked to Russia’s ‘dark fleet’
asia-pacific.summary:
Asia’s fuel needs may take priority over the strategic risks of relying on oil from heavily sanctioned Russia, according to experts.
Currently, there is little political appetite to treat the surge of Moscow’s crude flowing eastward through a “dark fleet” of tankers as a potential security issue.
The US-Israeli war on Iran has caused the price of Brent crude – the benchmark measure – to spike to above US$100 since March, although talks with Washington have seen the price soften in recent days to

asia-pacific.title: Asia’s ‘NewSpace’ economy is about more than just exploration
asia-pacific.summary:
For decades, Asia’s ambitions in outer space were closely associated with scientific discovery, technological prestige and national symbolism. Space programmes were designed to demonstrate engineering capability, economic modernisation and geopolitical influence.
Today, outer space is undergoing a deeper transformation. It is no longer viewed merely as a frontier for exploration; increasingly, it is seen as a strategic domain tied to sovereignty, economic security and geopolitical...

asia-pacific.title: Taiwan targets Beijing’s grey-zone tactics near remote South China Sea islands
asia-pacific.summary:
Taiwan’s navy will support patrols around the Taipei-controlled Dongsha Islands after mainland Chinese coastguard activity near the South China Sea atoll surged over the past year.
The activity has fuelled concerns in Taipei that Beijing is using the remote outpost to test Taiwan’s responses and refine its grey-zone tactics.
Taiwan’s Coast Guard Administration said mainland Chinese coastguard vessels had appeared around the atoll 39 times since February last year, compared with only occasional..

asia-pacific.title: China boy with 97% burns dreams of becoming top badminton player, practises daily despite pain
asia-pacific.summary:
A boy in eastern China who survived catastrophic burns is battling daily pain on the badminton court, determined to become a champion like his idol Lin Dan.
Xuanxuan, 12, from Jinan in Shandong province, was asleep with his family when a fire tore through his parents’ small noodle shop six years ago, Qilu Evening News reported.
He suffered the worst injuries, with burns covering 97 per cent of his body, while his parents and younger sister were also injured.
His mother told the mainland media...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong leader’s visit set to unlock Kazakh flights, with incentives eyed
asia-pacific.summary:
A lack of direct flights has been a bugbear to Hong Kong and Kazakhstan forging closer ties but with the city’s leader now in Astana and hints of incentives on the table, hopes are rising for a breakthrough.
It is understood that a Hong Kong-based airline is now exploring launching such flights up to three times a week, but the details are being ironed out.
Asel Suankulova, managing director of Kazakh Invest’s Investment Promotion and Marketing Department, told the South China Morning Post that.

asia-pacific.title: ‘Reality check’ for Japan as Britain’s funding shortfall threatens fighter jet project
asia-pacific.summary:
Japan’s patience with Britain as a defence partner is being tested after reports that funding shortfalls in London could delay a next-generation fighter jet that both countries are developing with Italy.
The Global Combat Air Programme, or GCAP, is intended to deliver a new fighter for the three countries by 2035, but The Telegraph reported on Saturday that the aircraft could be delayed by several years because of uncertainty over Britain’s defence spending plans.
The report has raised concerns.

asia-pacific.title: Senators blast Trump for allowing AI chips to be sent to overseas units of Chinese firms
asia-pacific.summary:
Democratic Senators Elizabeth Warren and Andy Kim on Monday slammed the Trump administration for potentially allowing advanced American AI chips to ‌be sent to overseas units of Chinese firms, and called on Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick to testify to Congress on the issue, according to a statement.
In a surprise move, the Department of ⁠Commerce, which oversees US exports, on Sunday issued guidance to ‌close a potential loophole that may have led companies to export the world’s most advanced

asia-pacific.title: China urges Brazil to jointly repel ‘external challenges’ as ties deepen in Beijing
asia-pacific.summary:
China’s top diplomat said on Monday that Beijing and Brasilia should “jointly repel external challenges”, as the two countries opened a high-level strategic dialogue in Beijing.
Foreign Minister Wang Yi made the remark during a visit by his Brazilian counterpart, Mauro Vieira, who arrived in the Chinese capital this weekend for the fifth Global Strategic Dialogue between the two governments.
“Practical cooperation in all areas has been steadily enhanced, and the two peoples have never been so...

asia-pacific.title: Philippines’ bet on shaky investment pledges from the US risks China’s ire
asia-pacific.summary:
“You have absolutely no idea of what you’re talking about. The whole point of Pax Silica is to partner with countries who are good at doing different things because everyone wins from a secure supply chain,” wrote US Undersecretary of State for Economic Affairs Jacob Helberg on a social media website in response to an online critic of the US strategic initiative with the Philippines.
The spirited exchange came after Manila’s reported rejection of a request to extend diplomatic immunity to US...

asia-pacific.title: Trump’s US$1.8 billion ‘weaponisation’ fund on hold after fierce opposition from Congress
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump’s US$1.8 billion ⁠fund to compensate victims of alleged government “weaponisation” has been put on hold after the White House faced fierce opposition from Republicans in Congress, three sources familiar with the plan said on Monday.
The extraordinary rebuke of Trump underscored an increased appetite to flex political power among some Republican senators against the president’s actions, particularly after his endorsement against two of ‌their Senate colleagues ahead of a
